name: Papa Bruno
subtitle: Sicilian Home Cooking
tagline: Nonna's recipes from Catania — hand-rolled pasta, wood-fired focaccia, and sardines a beccafico, a short walk from Westminster.
registeredAddress: 47 Marsham Street, London SW1P 3DP, United Kingdom
pageTitle: Papa Bruno — Sicilian Restaurant in Westminster, London
menuIntroText: Hand-rolled pasta, wood-fired focaccia, and recipes Bruno's nonna taught him in Catania. Honest Sicilian cooking, no shortcuts.

items.name: Pasta alla Norma
items.desc: Rigatoni, fried aubergine, tomato, and salted ricotta — the dish of Sicily

items.name: Sardine a Beccafico
items.desc: Fresh sardines stuffed with breadcrumbs, pine nuts, and raisins, baked in the Sicilian way

items.name: Spaghetti alle Vongole
items.desc: Clams, garlic, white wine, and a whisper of chilli

items.name: Involtini di Vitello
items.desc: Veal rolls with provola, parsley, and lemon, slow-braised in white wine

items.name: Focaccia di Bruno
items.desc: Wood-fired focaccia with cherry tomato, oregano, and Sicilian olive oil

items.name: Cannoli Siciliani
items.desc: Crispy shells filled with sweet ricotta, pistachio, and candied orange

items.name: Cassata
items.desc: Sponge, ricotta, marzipan, and candied fruit — Easter in a slice

reservationNote: We release tables 30 days in advance. Pre-theatre sittings before 18:00 fill fastest — book early for Westminster evenings.
footerNote: Family recipes from Catania, served on Marsham Street since 1998.
aboutParagraphs: Bruno left a tiny kitchen in Catania for London in 1998, carrying his nonna's recipe book and a dream of a proper Sicilian table. What started as twelve covers is now one of Westminster's most-loved neighbourhood restaurants.
We make everything by hand — our pasta is rolled daily, our bread baked in a wood-fired oven, and every sauce built from scratch.
No shortcuts. No fusion. Just the food Bruno grew up with, on a quiet street near the Abbey.

whyChooseItems.text: Pasta rolled fresh each morning — never dried, never bought in
5★ Food Hygiene — inspected every six months
Fully licensed — Sicilian wine by the carafe or the bottle
540+ five-star reviews — check our Google profile

reviews.name: James Whitfield
reviews.text: The sardines a beccafico are the best thing I've eaten in London this year. Quiet, unpretentious, perfect.

reviews.name: Sofia Romano
reviews.text: Tastes exactly like my nonna's kitchen in Palermo. Bruno came out to say hello — proper Sicilian hospitality.

reviews.name: The Okafors
reviews.text: Took the in-laws for a birthday lunch. Cannoli worth the trip alone. We'll be back.
